Our Story

Born from a love of real indulgence.

Meltt began with one stubborn belief — that a dessert should feel like an event. Not an afterthought. Every sundae we build is a layered experience: textures that surprise, flavours that linger, portions that never disappoint.

We are Bangalore's home of the sundae. A place where late evenings get sweeter and ordinary nights become something worth remembering.

30+
Items on menu
4.9★
Google rating
"After all, what good is an day without something worth melting for."
What We Serve

Every dish is a reason to visit.

From our legendary Death by Chocolate to the golden Dubai Kunafa Sundae — each creation is built layer by layer, fresh every day.

Signature Sundaes · Best Seller

Death By Chocolate.

A deadly combination of chocolate cake, vanilla ice cream topped with hot chocolate sauce, cream and roasted peanuts. The dish that started everything at Meltt — and the reason most people keep coming back.

Bangalore's most indulgent sundae. Served fresh every day, without exception.

Must Try
Fudge Fantasy

Spl. Mocha Hot Fudge.

Vanilla and mocha ice cream sitting in a pool of hot chocolate sauce, crowned with roasted peanuts. Served hot and cold at the same time — that contrast is the whole trick. One of those dishes that makes you close your eyes on the first spoon.

Hot & Cold
Layered Sundaes

Oreo & Biscoff Sundae.

Two iconic biscuits. Two completely different flavours. One cup. The dark chocolate crumble of Oreo sits next to the warm caramel sweetness of Biscoff — somehow they work perfectly together. Impossible to choose which is better, so we put both in one sundae.

Two in One
Choco Treats

The Pudding Affair.

Walnut chocolate bread pudding served warm with hot custard. Rich, deep, and exactly what you needed without knowing it. A Meltt original that quietly became the order that gets recommended table to table, every single day.

Warm Served
The Spread

Made to be stared at.

Every creation at Meltt is built layer by layer — because eating with your eyes first is half the experience.
